relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Elihu asks Job to hear him directly.
His mouth opens with urgency.
He claims upright heart and clear knowledge.
He names spirit and breath as the source of his life.
He invites Job to answer if able.
He stands as another clay-made human before God.
He says Job need not fear him as he fears the Divine.
Elihu has heard Job’s words.
He summarizes Job’s claim of cleanness.
He summarizes Job’s sense that God treats him as enemy.
He names Job’s feeling of being watched and restrained.
Elihu says Job is not right here because God exceeds humanity.
He asks why Job contends as though God never answers.
God speaks, Elihu says, though humans may not perceive it.
Dream and night vision become one channel of speech.
The ear is opened and instruction sealed.
The purpose is to turn a person from pride and deed.
This speech can keep a soul from the pit.
Pain on the bed can also become correction in Elihu’s view.
The body loses desire for bread and pleasant food.
Flesh wastes and bones show.
The soul nears the pit.
A rare messenger-mediator may declare what is right.
Grace may speak ransom from the pit.
Restoration is imagined as renewed youth.
Prayer, accepted face, joy, and restored righteousness follow.
The restored one confesses wrong and mercy.
The soul is redeemed from the pit to see light.
Elihu says the Divine works this way again and again.
The soul is brought back to the light of life.
Elihu asks Job for attention and silence.
If Job has words, Elihu invites them.
If not, Elihu will continue teaching wisdom.
